metadata.landingTitle: Visual AI Workflow Builder for Creators and Teams
metadata.landingDescription: Build AI workflows for prompts, image generation, video analysis, and reusable production pipelines in one visual canvas.
metadata.exploreDescription: Discover public AI workflows for image generation, video pipelines, prompt chains, and creator automation.
metadata.workflowsDescription: Browse reusable AI workflow templates for prompt engineering, image generation, video production, and multimodal automation.
metadata.videoAnalysisDescription: Upload videos and use AI to extract storyboards, scripts, shot notes, and production insights.

landingSeo.title: One visual AI workflow builder for prompt systems, image pipelines, and video analysis
landingSeo.intro: Nano Banana Canvas helps creators and teams design repeatable AI workflows in one visual canvas. Map prompts, connect models, test GPT Image 2 prompt systems, share reusable templates, and keep production logic readable from idea to delivery.
landingSeo.coverageBody: The current product is built for distributed creator teams that need one shared workflow surface across time zones, languages, and production contexts.
landingSeo.coverageRegionAmericas: North America and remote US creator teams
landingSeo.coverageRegionEurope: European studios, agencies, and independent creators
landingSeo.coverageRegionApac: Asia-Pacific production teams and bilingual operators
landingSeo.capabilityBody: Use one canvas to connect prompt planning, image generation, video analysis, and reusable workflow templates without hiding logic inside fragmented tools.
landingSeo.geoBody: We describe real service scope, supported team setups, and working regions directly on the page instead of mass-producing thin location pages that only swap place names.
landingSeo.geoNote: That keeps the site aligned with Google guidance while still helping search engines and answer engines understand where the product is useful.
landingSeo.faqQuestion1: What is Nano Banana Canvas used for?
landingSeo.faqAnswer1: Nano Banana Canvas is a visual AI workflow builder for creators and teams who need reusable prompt chains, image generation steps, video analysis, and publishable workflow templates in one workspace.
landingSeo.faqQuestion2: Who is this workflow builder for?
landingSeo.faqAnswer2: It is designed for filmmakers, creative studios, agencies, and independent operators who need to coordinate AI-assisted production across different tools and time zones.
landingSeo.faqQuestion3: Does the platform support global teams?
landingSeo.faqAnswer3: Yes. The current product positioning explicitly supports distributed teams across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, with English and Chinese product messaging available today.
landingSeo.structuredDescription: Visual AI workflow builder for creators and teams. Build prompt systems, image pipelines, video analysis flows, and reusable templates in one canvas.
landingSeo.featureWorkflow: Visual workflow builder for prompt systems and creator operations
landingSeo.featureImageVideo: Image generation, GPT Image 2 prompt systems, video analysis, and multimodal production pipelines
landingSeo.featureTemplates: Reusable workflow templates for teams and community sharing

hero.heading: Nano Banana Canvas
hero.description: Build cinematic stories on an infinite canvas. Connect image, video, LLM, GPT Image 2 prompt experiments, and 3D models in one visual workflow — from first prompt to final cut.
hero.models: The visual AI platform for filmmakers and creators.

models.title: Model Support Orbital Cloud Map
models.body: Connect leading AI model providers inside one visual system and keep expanding over time. Image, video, text, audio, and routing layers can be switched, compared, and chained on the same canvas, so multi-model production feels orchestrated instead of scattered.

vendors.label: Connected providers
vendors.body: Coverage across image, video, text, audio, and routing layers.

coverage.label: Unified orchestration
coverage.body: Different model families move through one visible workflow.

routing.label: Cross-model handoff
routing.body: Switches, branches, loops, and comparisons stay readable.

team.label: Continuous experience
team.body: The public site and the signed-in workflow share one coherent story.

features.title: Built Around How You Actually Work
features.body: Whether you’re a solo filmmaker or a studio team, Nano Banana Canvas turns fragmented AI tools into one creative flow.

models.title: Auto-execution
models.body: Batch generate, refine, and regenerate in one flow.

outputs.title: Custom logic
outputs.body: Loops, conditional branches, and parallel runs.

pricing.title: From First Frame to Final Cut
pricing.body: Three purchase modes designed for ongoing production, project-based delivery, and fast credit top-ups.

free.name: Free
free.body: All plans include the infinite canvas and visual workflow builder.

standard.name: Standard
standard.body: Adds more model calls and room for consistent weekly output.

pro.name: Pro
pro.body: Adds more team seats plus 4K export headroom for active production.

ultimate.name: Ultimate
ultimate.body: Adds the heaviest model volume, team capacity, and 8K-oriented export room.

pricing.summaryNote: Auto monthly plans fit steady production, one-time plans fit project-based buying, and credit packs fit fast top-ups during busy runs.

monthly.body: Standard, Pro, and Ultimate monthly plans for teams that produce continuously.

oneTime.body: The same Standard, Pro, and Ultimate tiers are available as one-time purchases with no automatic renewal.

credits.body: Four credit packs: 500, 1200, 3500, and 8000 credits for surges, deadlines, and temporary capacity.

testimonials.title: What Filmmakers and Creators Are Saying
testimonials.body: Real notes from filmmakers, visual artists, and creative technologists building on one infinite canvas.

pixel.quote: Finally, a canvas that doesn’t limit my imagination. Nano Banana Canvas turns scattered ideas into visual flows. Absolutely next-level.

lena.quote: The cinematic quality I get from connecting Midjourney + Pika on one infinite board is insane. My moodboard-to-shot pipeline just got 10x faster. Future of previs.

prompt.quote: No more tab switching. I link SDXL, Runway, and ElevenLabs in one workspace. The “creative flow” is real. Nano Banana Canvas is a spatial computer for storytellers.

frame.quote: Enterprise-grade AI orchestration. The node-based workflow on an infinite canvas feels like Unreal for prompt chains. Indispensable.

neo.quote: I was skeptical. Then I built a 4-shot storyboard, animated each frame, and rendered a rough cut – all inside one infinite canvas. Nano Banana Canvas is legit.

moodboard.quote: My ideas usually die in scattered notes. Now they flow visually. Connecting LLMs to image generators on a never-ending canvas? That’s the creative OS I’ve waited for.

indie.quote: Finally a tool that respects my messiness. Infinite canvas, infinite possibilities. Nano Banana Canvas feels like the future of filmmaking.

workflow.quote: Pros: real-time node linking, 4K exports, low latency. Connects to ComfyUI, Kling, Pika, and GPT-4o. Cons: none yet. This is how AI for creators should work.

cyber.quote: Imagine Miro + Unreal Engine + ComfyUI had a baby. That’s Nano Banana Canvas. The “film sense” is baked in – even my rough generations look cinematic.

canvas.quote: Infinite canvas. Connected models. Pure creative flow. Thank you, Nano Banana Canvas. My burnouts are gone.

vfx.quote: As a solo creator, I need one space to sketch, prompt, generate, and refine. Nano Banana Canvas gives me that. The ability to drag a video output into a new prompt chain – on the same infinite board – changed my pre-production completely. Feels professional, not gimmicky.

dream.quote: This is not just another AI wrapper. It’s a spatial workflow for visual storytellers. Nano Banana Canvas understands: creativity doesn’t live in linear timelines. It lives on an infinite canvas. 10/10.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.body: Everything creators usually want to know before moving real image, video, and workflow production into Nano Banana Canvas.

what.question: What is Nano Banana Canvas?
what.answer: A visual AI platform where creators connect different models (image, video, LLM, 3D) on an infinite canvas to turn ideas into workflows — designed specifically for filmmakers and digital storytellers.

models.question: Which AI models can I orchestrate here?
models.answer: You can map image, video, language, vision, and audio providers in one place, including OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Runway, Kling, Qwen, Wan, Black Forest, and other routing layers you already use.

canvas.question: Do I need to code to build workflows?
canvas.answer: No. Drag, drop, branch, and sequence steps visually. The canvas is meant to feel closer to a storyboard wall than a developer console.

gptImage.question: Can I use Nano Banana Canvas for GPT Image 2 workflows?
gptImage.answer: Yes. Many teams use the canvas to map GPT Image 2 prompt studies, review reference images, and connect those outputs into broader image-to-video or prompt-to-story workflows.

pricing.question: Can I bring my own API keys?
pricing.answer: Yes. The product is designed to support both hosted execution paths and bring-your-own-key setups, so teams can keep flexibility around cost, routing, and provider choice.

api.question: How is pricing structured?
api.answer: Pricing is split between Free, recurring plans, one-time plans, and credit packs. The public pricing page explains the billing boundary clearly, and checkout stays on Stripe.

team.question: Is this only for solo creators?
team.answer: No. The product is framed for solo operators, creative studios, and distributed teams that need one shared canvas for prompts, assets, and handoff notes.

commercial.question: Can I use generated output in commercial production?
commercial.answer: Commercial use depends on the providers and assets in your workflow, but the platform is built to help teams keep those steps visible instead of burying them across disconnected tools.

privacy.question: How do privacy and account-scoped settings work?
privacy.answer: Account-scoped configuration is isolated to the signed-in user context, while public legal pages explain how billing, cookies, and privacy are handled at the product level.

contact.question: Where can I find docs, community, or contact channels?
contact.answer: Use the public Docs, Community, and Contact pages from the top navigation or Footer. They exist as standalone landing pages so search engines and teams can reach the right context directly.

cta.title: Build your next cinematic workflow on one infinite canvas.
cta.body: Bring image, video, language, and 3D models into a single visual flow that stays readable from first prompt to final cut.

footer.copyright: © 2026 Nano Banana Canvas. All rights reserved.

pricing.metaDescription: View Nano Banana Canvas subscription pricing with live Stripe-backed prices and region-aware currencies.
pricing.title: Choose the plan that matches your production volume
pricing.description: Plan prices are loaded from Stripe at request time so the UI stays aligned with the real billing configuration instead of drifting behind hard-coded numbers.
pricing.livePriceNote: Displayed currency is resolved automatically from your region and Stripe price configuration.
pricing.pricingUnavailable: Paid prices cannot be loaded from Stripe right now. You can still use the Free entry point; paid checkout will return when billing configuration is available.
pricing.freeEyebrow: Free is the default entry state
pricing.freeTitle: Start on Free first. Upgrade only when your production volume actually grows.
pricing.freeDescription: Free is not a checkout product. It is the account state you land in by default, so you can enter the canvas, validate the workflow, and only move into paid plans when monthly credits or storage become the real bottleneck.
pricing.freeFeatureEntryBody: Every new account starts here by default.
pricing.freeFeatureCreditsBody: Upgrade only when credits or storage become the constraint.
pricing.freeFeatureUpgradeBody: There is no “buy Free” button because Free is not a Stripe SKU.
pricing.billingNoticeBody: Auto monthly plans renew until you cancel. One-time plans and credit packs are charged once and do not create a recurring subscription.
pricing.refundNoticeBody: Unused one-time plans or credit packs are not promised to be refundable by default. Any exception is handled case by case under our Terms and Stripe payment records.
pricing.currencyNoticeBody: Displayed currency is resolved from your region and Stripe configuration, but the final charge currency and amount shown in Stripe Checkout at payment time control the order.
pricing.standardDescription: For solo creators who need a stable monthly credit pool and room for production assets.
pricing.proDescription: For active operators and small teams shipping more image, video, and workflow output each month.
pricing.ultimateDescription: For heavier production teams that need a much larger credit budget and longer storage runway.

features.metaTitle: Feature Details for Visual AI Workflow Production
features.metaDescription: Explore Nano Banana Canvas feature details for visual workflow orchestration, GPT Image 2 prompt mapping, multimodal production, and team-ready operating flows.
features.heroTitle: A visual operating layer for image, video, and prompt production.
features.heroBody: The landing page introduces the product. This page explains how the workflow layer actually behaves once you start mapping prompts, image systems, video tools, and reusable production logic across a single canvas.

1.title: One shared surface
1.body: Keep prompts, references, routing logic, and asset handoff visible in one place instead of splitting them across tabs.

2.title: SEO-ready detail page
2.body: This route exists to give search and answer engines a stable page for capability-level queries.

3.title: Built for production operators
3.body: Use it for storyboard systems, look-dev passes, GPT Image 2 prompt studies, and image-to-video chains.

orchestration.title: Map the flow, not just the prompt.
orchestration.body: The product is strongest when the logic stays visible. Instead of hiding decisions in chat history or scattered notebooks, you keep the workflow legible as a sequence of connected media operations.

canvas.title: Spatial workflow composition
canvas.body: Lay out prompt planning, reference gathering, generation passes, and review nodes on one surface so the relationship between steps stays obvious.

branching.title: Readable conditions and alternates
branching.body: Keep multiple image directions, branch-specific prompts, and fallback paths visible instead of burying them in duplicated documents.

handoff.title: From one model to the next
handoff.body: Treat each provider as one stage in a larger creative chain, whether you are moving from text planning to images or from still frames into motion.

production.title: Keep the image, video, and asset chain connected.
production.body: Nano Banana Canvas is not a single generator. It is the operating layer that lets you move through multiple generation stages without losing the context behind each output.

image.title: Prompt systems for image generation
image.body: Use one board to compare GPT Image 2 prompt structures, Qwen image directions, Black Forest look-dev passes, and reference image notes.

bullets.1: Compare subject, style, and lighting changes side by side.
bullets.2: Keep source references and prompt revisions attached to the same visual step.
bullets.3: Hand the selected frame cleanly into a downstream motion workflow.

video.title: Image-to-video sequencing
video.body: Take approved still frames into Kling, Wan, Runway, or other motion systems while keeping the earlier prompt logic visible.

bullets.1: Track which still image led to which motion output.
bullets.2: Keep duration, camera intent, and scene notes readable near the node.
bullets.3: Reduce guesswork when the team needs to regenerate a shot.

library.title: Templates that stay operational
library.body: Turn working flows into reusable systems for concept art, ad iteration, campaign look-dev, or previs without flattening the creative context.

bullets.1: Use templates as starting systems instead of blank boards.
bullets.2: Share operating patterns with the team without forcing a rigid pipeline.
bullets.3: Keep the logic understandable for whoever opens the workflow later.

team.title: Designed for people who need to ship, not just experiment.
team.body: The best workflows are easy to reopen, explain, and continue. This page exists because capability pages should help operators understand how the product fits real production rhythms.

roles.title: Readable by multiple roles
roles.body: Creative leads, prompt operators, and editors can follow the same flow without reverse-engineering what happened.

ops.title: Less tab chaos
ops.body: Reduce the handoff loss that happens when prompt docs, image boards, and output references live in different tools.

handover.title: Feature detail pages that explain the product
handover.body: Dedicated feature pages give search engines a stronger route for product understanding than one crowded homepage alone.

footerCta.title: See how the feature layer connects to pricing and model support.
footerCta.body: Use the public page tree to understand the product by topic, then move into the canvas only when you are ready to test the workflow for real.

models.metaTitle: AI Model Directory for Image, Video, Audio, and Prompt Workflows
models.metaDescription: Browse the Nano Banana Canvas AI model directory, including GPT Image 2 workflow mapping, OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Kling, Runway, Wan, Qwen, and more.
models.heroTitle: A model layer built for orchestration, not logo collecting.
models.heroBody: This page explains the role each model family plays inside Nano Banana Canvas. The goal is not to list brands for decoration. The goal is to show how image, video, language, audio, and routing systems can live inside one readable workflow.

1.title: Image, video, text, audio
1.body: The product frames model choice as part of the workflow, not a separate shopping list.

2.title: Prompt-system friendly
2.body: Use the canvas to compare prompt structures, route image outputs, and hold references beside the actual generation step.

3.title: Includes GPT Image 2 intent
3.body: Queries around GPT Image 2, image workflow design, and multimodal orchestration now land on a dedicated public page.

images.title: Image generation systems that feed a larger production chain.
images.body: Image models matter most when they plug into something larger: storyboards, campaign look-dev, asset packs, motion prep, and repeatable prompting systems.

gptImage.title: GPT Image 2 prompt tracks
gptImage.body: Use the canvas to map subject prompts, reference images, composition changes, and downstream approvals around GPT Image 2 style image generation research.

bullets.1: Track which prompt revision produced the keeper frame.
bullets.2: Keep reference imagery visible beside the generation step.
bullets.3: Hand the result into video tools without losing the prompt context.

flux.title: Look-dev with Black Forest image models
flux.body: Organize lighting, texture, and style variants while keeping the broader workflow understandable to the rest of the team.

bullets.1: Compare style families in parallel branches.
bullets.2: Keep model-specific notes near the actual node.

qwen.title: Regional and multilingual image pipelines
qwen.body: When a workflow needs multilingual prompting or region-specific image tooling, Qwen-style image systems fit into the same board instead of becoming another isolated stack.

bullets.1: Mix bilingual prompting with shared references.
bullets.2: Keep revisions transparent for cross-functional review.
bullets.3: Move selected frames cleanly into motion generation.

video.title: From still frames to motion systems.
video.body: Kling, Runway, Wan, and similar tools become more useful when the still frame, prompt logic, and motion note are documented in one place.

runway.title: Shot-level motion design
runway.body: Capture scene intent, camera notes, and look-dev choices before they disappear into a single prompt box.

kling.title: Image-to-video continuity
kling.body: Use Kling stages where continuity and shot carry-over matter, while keeping the earlier frame choice visible beside the motion pass.

wan.title: Structured video experimentation
wan.body: Use Wan-style steps for experiments that benefit from multiple input conditions, then route the result into review or follow-up generation.

platform.title: Language, routing, vision, and operations are part of the same system.
platform.body: A real production workflow rarely stops at one generator. It includes language planning, routing layers, reference analysis, and reusable logic for the next run.

llm.title: Prompt planning and script logic
llm.body: Use OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, or other language providers as planning and rewrite stages around visual production.

routing.title: Model selection without losing clarity
routing.body: Services like OpenRouter can sit inside the workflow as routing layers rather than becoming invisible configuration.

ops.title: Asset context next to each node
ops.body: Store references, timing notes, and handoff logic next to the model step that actually uses them.

vision.title: Image understanding as workflow fuel
vision.body: Vision models help extract details, annotate references, and keep the workflow aware of the source image instead of starting blind.

footerCta.title: Move from model awareness to workflow design.
footerCta.body: Read the feature page if you need to understand how these models connect operationally, then step into the canvas when you want to test the flow for yourself.

about.metaDescription: Learn what Nano Banana Canvas is building, who it is for, and why the product is organized around visual AI workflow clarity instead of fragmented tool-hopping.
about.heroTitle: We are building a calmer operating layer for modern AI production.
about.heroBody: Nano Banana Canvas exists because image, video, and prompt work usually gets split across too many tools, too many tabs, and too much hidden context. We want the workflow itself to stay visible, reusable, and understandable.

1.title: Built for creators and operators
1.body: The product is aimed at filmmakers, creative studios, independent operators, and teams who need process clarity.

2.title: Product-first public pages
2.body: This marketing tree exists so every major topic has a dedicated, searchable page instead of being buried on the homepage.

3.title: Workflow taste matters
3.body: We care about visual quality, but also about whether a workflow still makes sense a week later when someone else opens it.

principles.title: The product follows three simple design beliefs.
principles.body: Good workflow software should reduce ambiguity, not just add another glossy surface on top of complexity.

clarity.title: Keep the logic visible
clarity.body: Prompts, references, asset notes, and generation paths should stay readable in the same workspace.

systems.title: Make good work reusable
systems.body: A great workflow should become a reusable operating system, not a one-off heroic effort that dies in screenshots.

taste.title: Calm interfaces create better judgment
taste.body: The interface should stay deliberate and spacious enough for teams to evaluate work clearly.

build.title: Built for people who need to reopen and explain the work.
build.body: The product becomes more useful as more people touch the workflow, because the point is not just generating outputs. The point is making the system readable.

operators.title: Prompt and workflow specialists
operators.body: Use the canvas to keep experiments, references, and final operating paths in one place.

shared.title: Studios and distributed collaborators
shared.body: Share a workflow that can survive time zones, async review, and multiple media formats.

shipping.title: People who need to ship repeatedly
shipping.body: Repeatable image, video, and campaign systems matter more than one perfect output generated in isolation.

footerCta.title: Need the product map rather than the brand story?
footerCta.body: The docs and feature pages explain the workflow surface in more concrete terms, while the community page shows how public sharing fits into the wider site.

docs.metaDescription: Read the public product map for Nano Banana Canvas, including quick starts, feature orientation, model support, and workflow operating guidance.
docs.heroTitle: The public product map for getting oriented fast.
docs.heroBody: This docs page is a public-facing navigation layer. It helps operators, collaborators, and search engines understand where to start without forcing everything through one landing page.

1.title: Quick orientation
1.body: Jump to features, models, pricing, or community depending on the question you actually have.

2.title: Search-friendly structure
2.body: Public docs pages make it easier for specific product queries to land in the right context.

3.title: Human-readable first
3.body: The goal is fast understanding, not encyclopedic volume.

quickStart.title: Three good ways to enter the product story.
quickStart.body: Different visitors arrive with different intent. The public docs should respect that instead of flattening everyone into the same homepage path.

start.title: Enter the canvas directly
start.body: If you already know what you want, go straight into the product and test the workflow yourself.

models.title: Understand the model layer first
models.body: Use the model page when your first question is provider fit, GPT Image 2 mapping, or multimodal coverage.

pricing.title: Check the billing boundary
pricing.body: Use the pricing page when you need to understand Free, plans, credit packs, or Stripe-backed purchase modes.

operating.title: What the public docs are designed to explain.
operating.body: The goal is to describe how the public site is organized so new visitors can navigate by topic instead of by guesswork.

workflow.title: How the canvas is supposed to be used
workflow.body: Feature pages explain why the product is organized around visible workflow logic instead of isolated generators.

assets.title: How outputs move through the system
assets.body: Model pages explain how image, video, language, and reference systems connect into one production chain.

team.title: Who the product is meant to help
team.body: About, community, and contact pages explain the audience, operating posture, and public support channels.

footerCta.title: Ready to stop reading and test the workflow directly?
footerCta.body: The docs are meant to shorten orientation time. Once you know where the product fits, the canvas itself is the real proof.

community.metaDescription: Learn how Nano Banana Canvas community pages connect public workflows, creator sharing, published examples, and reusable operating patterns.
community.heroTitle: A public layer for shared workflows, examples, and creator momentum.
community.heroBody: The community route exists to explain how public workflows fit into the wider product. It helps visitors understand that the platform is not only for building privately, but also for sharing, studying, and reusing working systems.

1.title: Public examples
1.body: Community pages turn workflows into public references instead of leaving them trapped inside private production boards.

2.title: Reusable patterns
2.body: Good community examples are useful because they reveal how the workflow was structured, not only what it produced.

3.title: More than a gallery
3.body: The public layer supports discovery, critique, and faster onboarding for new operators.

share.title: Why a community layer matters for workflow software.
share.body: Publishing useful workflows is one of the fastest ways to teach a product category. It shows how other operators think, sequence, and recover from ambiguity.

explore.title: Explore public workflows
explore.body: Browse finished examples, inspect published work, and understand how other creators structure a production flow.

workflow.title: Start from working systems
workflow.body: Community workflows are most valuable when they act as launchpads instead of static inspiration boards.

feedback.title: Let feedback improve the operating pattern
feedback.body: Public sharing helps the best workflow structures surface faster than private trial-and-error alone.

participation.title: Use the public site tree to enter the right community surface.
participation.body: Some visitors want published examples. Others want reusable templates or direct contact. This page gives each of those routes a cleaner entry point.

operators.title: Browse published outputs
operators.body: Use the Explore route when you want public examples, creator context, and shareable work.

templates.title: See reusable workflow systems
templates.body: Use the workflow-sharing route when you want reusable operating structures instead of just browsing outputs.

support.title: Reach the team and public channels
support.body: Use the contact page if you need support, public channel entry points, or direct product questions.

footerCta.title: Need the people layer instead of the gallery layer?
footerCta.body: Move into contact or docs if you need direct support, product orientation, or a clearer read on how the public site connects to the private canvas.

refundPolicy.metaDescription: Review the Nano Banana Canvas refund policy for subscriptions, one-time plans, credit packs, and billing exceptions.
refundPolicy.heroTitle: A plain-language view of our refund boundary.
refundPolicy.heroBody: This page exists to explain the practical refund boundary in public. It does not replace the Terms of Service, but it gives teams a cleaner place to understand how recurring plans, one-time plans, and credit packs are treated.

1.title: Recurring plans
1.body: Auto-monthly subscriptions stay active through the paid period after cancellation.

2.title: One-time billing value
2.body: One-time plans and credit packs are generally treated as delivered digital value once provisioned.

3.title: Exceptions exist
3.body: Duplicate charges and technical provisioning failures can still be reviewed case by case.

rules.title: The four situations most people actually care about.
rules.body: The public refund page should clarify the big billing cases without forcing users to scan long legal text just to understand the core boundary.

subscriptions.title: Recurring subscriptions
subscriptions.body: If you cancel a recurring plan, the plan remains active until the paid billing period ends.

bullets.1: The current paid cycle is generally not retroactively refunded.
bullets.2: Future renewal is stopped once the cancellation takes effect.

oneTime.title: One-time plan purchases
oneTime.body: One-time plans deliver a bundled billing value rather than a recurring subscription contract.

bullets.2: They are generally treated as consumed digital value once provisioned.

creditPacks.title: Permanent credit purchases
creditPacks.body: Credit packs exist for capacity top-ups and are handled separately from subscription state.

bullets.1: They are usually not refundable after successful provisioning.
bullets.2: They do not change your plan tier by themselves.

exceptions.title: Billing exceptions
exceptions.body: We may review refunds for edge cases where the billing event clearly failed the intended product outcome.

bullets.1: Examples include duplicate charges or technical entitlement failures.
bullets.2: Review depends on Stripe records and the specific account history.

acceptableUse.metaDescription: Read the Nano Banana Canvas acceptable use policy covering safe workflow publishing, provider compliance, and prohibited misuse.
acceptableUse.heroTitle: Use the canvas creatively, but keep the operating boundary clear.
acceptableUse.heroBody: This page summarizes the main usage boundary in a way that product teams, creators, and collaborators can understand quickly before they start publishing or sharing workflows.

1.title: Provider rules still apply
1.body: You remain responsible for following the rules of the model providers used inside your workflow.

2.title: Public sharing matters
2.body: Published workflows and assets should be lawful, non-abusive, and safe to surface on a public platform.

3.title: Visibility helps accountability
3.body: The product is designed to keep context visible, which also helps teams notice risky use sooner.

rules.title: What is welcome, restricted, and expected.
rules.body: A strong acceptable use page does not try to cover every imaginable abuse case. It explains the operating boundary that most users need in plain language.

allowed.title: Creative and commercial workflow building
allowed.body: You may build, test, organize, and publish lawful workflows for creative development, production planning, and approved commercial work.

bullets.1: Use the product for storyboards, campaign systems, look-dev, and process design.
bullets.2: Share reusable workflows when you have the right to share the inputs and outputs.
bullets.3: Use public pages and contact routes responsibly.

restricted.title: Abuse, deception, or unlawful harm
restricted.body: You may not use the platform to create or distribute unlawful, deceptive, exploitative, or abusive workflows and content.

bullets.1: Do not publish material that violates law, privacy, or third-party rights.
bullets.2: Do not use the service to facilitate harassment, fraud, or platform abuse.
bullets.3: Do not bypass provider, billing, or account restrictions through misuse.

sharing.title: Public workflows should stay reviewable
sharing.body: The public community layer works best when shared workflows are understandable, safe, and honest about what they do.

bullets.1: Add enough context for others to understand the published workflow.
bullets.2: Do not misrepresent generated output as something it is not.
bullets.3: Expect published assets to be removed if they create safety or compliance risk.

cookies.metaDescription: Learn how Nano Banana Canvas uses cookies and related storage for authentication, analytics, preferences, and checkout continuity.
cookies.heroTitle: A public explanation of the cookie categories that keep the site working.
cookies.heroBody: This page explains the role cookies and related browser storage play across sign-in, analytics, and product continuity. It is meant to complement the privacy page with a faster, more direct summary.

1.title: Essential cookies
1.body: Required for account continuity, sign-in, and secure product access.

2.title: Analytics signals
2.body: Used to understand public site and product usage patterns so the team can improve the experience.

3.title: Preference storage
3.body: Helps preserve settings such as locale or other browser-level experience choices.

categories.title: The categories most visitors need to understand.
categories.body: The cookie page should help visitors understand why certain browser storage exists before they decide what to allow or block.

essential.title: Authentication and secure continuity
essential.body: These cookies support sign-in, session continuity, and secure transitions through protected account features.

bullets.1: Blocking them can break login and account-scoped settings.
bullets.2: They also help protect checkout and other secure flows.

analytics.title: Usage understanding
analytics.body: Analytics cookies help the team understand what public pages and product areas are actually useful so they can improve navigation and content structure.

bullets.1: They are used for site and product improvement rather than ad-targeting narratives.
bullets.2: Exact implementation may evolve with the analytics stack.

preferences.title: Experience and locale preferences
preferences.body: Preference storage helps preserve a calmer product experience by remembering choices that affect how the site is displayed.

bullets.1: Examples include language selection or other low-risk display preferences.
bullets.2: Removing them may reset parts of the browsing experience.

auth.loginSubtitle: Enter your email and password to continue shaping workflows across devices.
auth.registerSubtitle: Create an account to enter your workspace, sync future assets, and manage account-scoped settings in one place.
auth.visualHeadline: Where scattered sparks become a living canvas.
auth.visualDescription: Step back into the night field of Nano Banana Canvas: ideas rise like quiet lanterns, catch their own reflection, and begin to move through image, motion, and memory.
auth.visualPointOne: Gather the first glimmer before it cools.
auth.visualPointTwo: Let images, prompts, and motion find one orbit.
auth.visualPointThree: Return to a workspace that remembers the shape of your imagination.
auth.termsPrefix: By creating an account you agree to our

profile.accountPageDesc: Manage profile, dashboard, subscriptions, works, notifications, and API connection cards for each capability.
profile.accountLoginRequiredTitle: Sign in to save account-level API configs
profile.accountLoginRequiredBody: These configs are now bound to your real account for cross-device access and per-user secret isolation. Guest sessions no longer write this kind of account asset.
profile.name: Name
profile.guestStatus: You are still in guest mode
profile.guestStatusBody: After signing in, your works, tasks, and account-level configs will be bound to your real account so visitor data does not bleed across users.
profile.signedInStatus: Your real account session is now active
profile.signedInStatusBody: From this point on, new account-level resources are bound to your real user record. Signing out returns you to a safe public page.
profile.manageBillingBody: If you already bought a plan, use this entry to open Stripe Customer Portal and manage your subscription, payment method, and billing details.
profile.profileSignedInDesc: Your account mirror, plan state, and credit assets are now connected to this workspace.
profile.profileSecurityBody: Password changes, account deletion, and other high-risk actions stay inside Clerk's security center instead of being fragmented into local forms.
profile.profileSecurityHintTitle: Password and deletion use one verified security flow
profile.profileSecurityHintBody: If this account was created through a social provider and no password exists yet, the security center will show an empty password state and guide the user through creating one. Account deletion is confirmed there as well.
profile.dashboardTitle: Keep plan state, credits, and runtime usage in one dashboard.
profile.dashboardDescription: Use this surface to review plan status, credit composition, monthly allowance burn, and the latest ledger detail before you upgrade, top up, or jump into Stripe management.
profile.dashboardUsageProgressBody: {used} / {total} credits consumed this month from the monthly pool.
profile.subscriptionTitle: Start with monthly plans, then decide whether you need one-time access or credit packs.
profile.subscriptionDescription: The default view always opens on monthly plans and Free remains the default account state. If your production is steady, Pro is highlighted as the most recommended option before you switch to one-time plans or credit packs.
profile.subscriptionModeMonthlyBody: Monthly plans fit continuous creation and steady output. Free remains the default entry state and Pro is highlighted as the recommended upgrade path.
profile.subscriptionModeOneTimeBody: One-time plans fit project-based buying. They avoid automatic renewal while keeping the same entitlement structure.
profile.subscriptionModeCreditsBody: Credit packs only add credits. They do not change your membership tier, which makes them useful for spikes and short bursts.
profile.subscriptionPricingUnavailable: Live Stripe pricing is temporarily unavailable. The Free path still works and paid checkout will return once billing configuration is restored.
profile.subscriptionTopUpFailed: Failed to create credit top-up checkout
profile.subscriptionCurrencyResolved: Checkout currency resolves to {currency} based on the current region and Stripe configuration.
profile.subscriptionFreeBody: Free is not a checkout product. It is the default state of every account so users can validate the workflow before deciding when to upgrade.
profile.subscriptionFreeMeta: Free is not a Stripe SKU, so this card only keeps the continue entry point.
profile.subscriptionPlanBody_standard: Built for individual creators who need a stable next step after Free and a reliable weekly production rhythm.
profile.subscriptionPlanBody_pro: Recommended for creators and small teams that are already producing at a steady pace and need the best balance of quota, storage, and price.
profile.subscriptionPlanBody_ultimate: Built for studio-scale workflows with heavier throughput, larger storage needs, and more demanding delivery cycles.
profile.apiConfigDesc: Maintain API connection configs for text, image, video, and audio. Nodes in API Key mode will read from these cards.
profile.apiConfigSecurityBody: These cards store API connection configs for each capability. Secrets are encrypted before they are written to the database and are read only within the current visitor context.
profile.noApiConfigs: No configs yet. Add one first.
profile.saveHint: After saving, matching nodes can use this config in API Key mode.
profile.capability_text_desc: Manage API providers and models used by text-generation nodes.
profile.capability_image_desc: Manage API providers and models used by image-generation nodes.
profile.capability_video_desc: Manage API providers and models used by video-generation nodes.
profile.capability_audio_desc: Manage API providers and models used by audio-generation nodes.

billing.title: Track credits, usage, and subscription state in one place
billing.description: This page reads from the local billing mirror so you can inspect balances, credit ledger history, and model usage before jumping into Stripe Customer Portal.
billing.balanceDescription: Your local billing mirror keeps monthly, permanent, and frozen credit pools separate so the account state stays auditable.
billing.historyDescription: Showing {shown} entries out of {total} credit ledger events.
billing.usageDescription: A {days}-day summary built from AI execution logs and model pricing snapshots.

profileWorks.localDraftBodyGuest: We found the local draft "{name}". Sign in to import it into your real account so it does not stay stranded on this device.
profileWorks.importDescription: Imported from the current device local draft
profileWorks.importSuccess: "{name}" has been imported into your account workspace
profileWorks.importFailed: Failed to import the local draft

contact.subtitle: Talk with the Nano Banana Canvas team and community across Telegram, Discord, X, and Instagram.
contact.telegramDesc: Join the Telegram channel for product updates and community discussion.
contact.discordDesc: Chat with creators, share workflows, and get help from the community.
contact.twitterDesc: Follow product announcements, roadmap notes, and release highlights.
contact.instagramDesc: See visual updates, creator showcases, and behind-the-scenes content.

legal.terms_acceptance_body: By accessing or using Nano Banana Canvas, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.
legal.terms_services_body: Nano Banana Canvas provides a visual AI workflow orchestration platform that allows users to build, share, and run AI-powered creative workflows. We reserve the right to modify or discontinue any part of the service at any time.
legal.terms_accounts_body: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials and for all activities under your account. You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account and keep it up to date.
legal.terms_content_body: You retain ownership of content you create using our platform. By publishing content, you grant us a non-exclusive license to display and distribute it within the platform. You are solely responsible for ensuring your content does not violate any laws or third-party rights.
legal.terms_access_body: We make reasonable efforts to keep the service available, but we do not guarantee permanent availability of any feature, model, or third-party provider. We may change, suspend, or remove capabilities for security, compliance, cost, or product reasons.
legal.terms_billing_body: Nano Banana Canvas offers a default Free account state, auto-monthly subscriptions, one-time plans, and one-time credit packs. Free is not a Stripe checkout product. Paid products only affect the billing entitlements we describe in-product, including monthly credits, permanent credits, and storage allowances.
legal.terms_refunds_body: Unless required by applicable law, auto-monthly subscriptions remain active through the paid billing period after cancellation and are not retroactively refunded for the current cycle. One-time plans and credit packs are generally treated as consumed digital billing value once provisioned to your account. Refund requests may be reviewed case by case for duplicate charges, technical provisioning failures, or other exceptional billing errors.
legal.terms_currency_body: Prices may be displayed in different currencies based on your region and Stripe price configuration. Those display values are informational until checkout. The final payable currency, taxes, and amount are the values shown in Stripe Checkout or the Stripe-hosted billing surface at the time you confirm payment.
legal.terms_termination_body: We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these terms. You may delete your account at any time. Upon termination, your data will be deleted in accordance with our Privacy Policy, while completed billing records may be retained for legal, accounting, fraud-prevention, or dispute-resolution purposes.
legal.terms_liability_body: Our platform is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service. Our total liability shall not exceed the amount you paid in the preceding 12 months.
legal.privacy_collection_body: We collect information you provide or generate while using the service, including workflow content, usage data, and technical data such as browser type, IP address, and device identifiers. When you configure account-scoped API keys, we store the minimum encrypted configuration data required to make that setup available across devices.
legal.privacy_usage_body: We use your information to provide and improve our services, process transactions, send service-related communications, prevent fraud, and comply with legal obligations. We do not sell your personal data to third parties.
legal.privacy_billing_body: When you start a paid checkout, we share the minimum data needed to create or manage the order with Stripe, such as your account identifier, billing plan or package selection, Stripe customer reference, and payment outcome metadata. We do not store raw card numbers on our own servers. Billing records, invoice references, and transaction identifiers may be retained to support entitlements, refunds, accounting, fraud prevention, and dispute handling.
legal.privacy_sharing_body: We share your information only with service providers who help us operate (such as Cloudflare and Stripe), when required by law, or when you choose to publish content publicly. Published workflows display your username and avatar.
legal.privacy_security_body: We implement industry-standard security measures including encryption in transit (TLS) and at rest. API keys used for account-scoped execution are encrypted before they are stored on the server and are only read within the current signed-in account context. We regularly review our security practices, but no networked service can promise absolute security.
legal.privacy_cookies_body: We use essential cookies for authentication, session management, and checkout continuity. We use analytics to understand how our platform is used. You can control cookie preferences through your browser settings, although disabling certain cookies may affect sign-in or billing flows.
legal.privacy_rights_body: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. You can export your workflows at any time. To delete your account and most associated data, visit your account settings or contact our support team. Some billing and compliance records may be retained when required by law or for legitimate business obligations.
legal.privacy_contact_body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at privacy@nanobanana.com. We will respond to your inquiry within 30 days.
